Name: Exeggcute
Flavor-Text: Using telepathy only fellow Exeggcute can pick up on, they always form a cluster of six.

Name: Exeggutor
Effects: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It is said that on rare occasions, one of its heads will drop off and continue on as an Exeggcute.

Name: Pinsir
Flavor-Text: These Pokémon judge one another based on pincers. Thicker, more impressive pincers make for more popularity with the opposite gender.

Name: Budew
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, they can’t play any Item cards from their hand.
Flavor-Text: The pollen it releases contains poison. If this Pokémon is raised on clean water, the poison’s toxicity is increased.

Name: Leafeon
Effects: Attach a Basic [G] Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Benched Pokémon. If you do, heal all damage from that Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The younger they are, the more they smell like fresh grass. With age, their fragrance takes on the odor of fallen leaves.

Name: Leafeon ex
Effects: This attack does 60 damage for each Energy attached to all of your opponent’s Pokémon.
Heal 100 damage from each of your Benched Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Tera-Rule: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s).

Name: Cottonee
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: It shoots cotton from its body to protect itself. If it gets caught up in hurricane-strength winds, it can get sent to the other side of the Earth.

Name: Whimsicott
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may heal all damage from your Active [G] Pokémon. If you healed any damage in this way, discard all Energy from that Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It scatters cotton all over the place as a prank. If it gets wet, it’ll become too heavy to move and have no choice but to answer for its mischief.

Name: Applin
Flavor-Text: Applin lives inside an apple. When an Applin is separated from its apple, its body loses moisture and the Pokémon grows weak.

Name: Dipplin
Ability-Effect: If Festival Grounds is in play, this Pokémon may use an attack it has twice. If the first attack Knocks Out your opponent’s Active Pokémon, you may attack again after your opponent chooses a new Active Pokémon.
Effects: This attack does 20 damage for each of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The head sticking out belongs to the fore-wyrm, while the tail belongs to the core-wyrm. The two share one apple and help each other out.

Name: Hydrapple ex
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may attach a Basic [G] Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon. If you attached Energy to a Pokémon in this way, heal 30 damage from that Pokémon.
Effects: This attack does 30 more damage for each [G] Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Teal Mask Ogerpon ex
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may attach a Basic [G] Energy card from your hand to this Pokémon. If you attached Energy to a Pokémon in this way, draw a card.
Effects: This attack does 30 more damage for each Energy attached to both Active Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Tera-Rule: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s).

Name: Flareon
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is a Pokémon ex or Pokémon V, this attack does 90 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Inhaled air is carried to its flame sac, heated, and exhaled as fire that reaches over 3,000 degrees Fahrenheit.

Name: Flareon ex
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Energy cards and attach them to 1 of your Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.
During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Tera-Rule: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s).

Name: Litleo
Flavor-Text: The more a Litleo trains its body and spirit by battling mighty enemies, the hotter its mane will grow.

Name: Pyroar
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
Flavor-Text: The mane of a male Pyroar heats up to over 3,600 degrees Fahrenheit during battle. Merely approaching it will cause severe burns.

Name: Hearthflame Mask Ogerpon ex
Effects: This attack does 20 damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.
If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is an Evolution Pokémon, this attack does 140 more damage, and discard all Energy from this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Tera-Rule: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s).

Name: Slowpoke
Flavor-Text: It is always vacantly lost in thought, but no one knows what it is thinking about. It is good at fishing with its tail.

Name: Slowking
Effects: You may put 2 Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon into their hand.
Flavor-Text: It has incredible intellect and intuition. Whatever the situation, it remains calm and collected.

Name: Goldeen
Ability-Effect: If Festival Grounds is in play, this Pokémon may use an attack it has twice. If the first attack Knocks Out your opponent’s Active Pokémon, you may attack again after your opponent chooses a new Active Pokémon.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its dorsal, pectoral, and tail fins wave elegantly in water. That is why it is known as the Water Dancer.

Name: Seaking
Ability-Effect: If Festival Grounds is in play, this Pokémon may use an attack it has twice. If the first attack Knocks Out your opponent’s Active Pokémon, you may attack again after your opponent chooses a new Active Pokémon.
Effects: Draw 2 cards.
Flavor-Text: In autumn, its body becomes more fatty in preparing to propose to a mate. It takes on beautiful colors.

Name: Vaporeon
Effects: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is a Pokémon ex or Pokémon V, this attack does 90 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It lives close to water. Its long tail is ridged with a fin, which is often mistaken for a mermaid’s.

Name: Vaporeon ex
Effects: This attack does 60 damage to each of your opponent’s Pokémon ex. This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness or Resistance.
During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Tera-Rule: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s).

Name: Suicune
Effects: This attack also does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It races around the world to purify fouled water. It dashes away with the north wind.

Name: Glaceon
Effects: At the end of your opponent’s next turn, put 9 damage counters on the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It lowers its body heat to freeze its fur. The hairs then become like needles it can fire.

Name: Glaceon ex
Effects: This attack also does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Knock Out 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon that has exactly 6 damage counters on it.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Tera-Rule: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s).

Name: Wellspring Mask Ogerpon ex
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon can’t retreat.

You may shuffle 3 Energy attached to this Pokémon into your deck. If you do, this attack also does 120 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Tera-Rule: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s).

Name: Pikachu ex
Effects: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Jolteon
Effects: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is a Pokémon ex or Pokémon V, this attack does 90 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It concentrates the weak electric charges emitted by its cells and launches wicked lightning bolts.

Name: Jolteon ex
Effects: You may discard up to 2 Basic Energy from your Benched Pokémon. This attack does 90 more damage for each card you discarded in this way.
During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Tera-Rule: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s).

Name: Iron Hands ex
Effects: If your opponent’s Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from this attack, take 1 more Prize card.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Iron Thorns ex
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, Pokémon with a Rule Box in play (both yours and your opponent’s) have no Abilities, except for Future Pokémon. (Pokémon ex, Pokémon V, etc. have Rule Boxes.)
Effects: Move an Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Espeon
Effects: This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: The tip of its forked tail quivers when it is predicting its opponent’s next move.

Name: Espeon ex
Effects: Discard a random card from your opponent’s hand.
Devolve each of your opponent’s evolved Pokémon by shuffling the highest Stage Evolution card on it into your opponent’s deck.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Tera-Rule: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s).

Name: Duskull
Effects: Put up to 3 Duskull from your discard pile onto your Bench.
Flavor-Text: Duskull’s victims suffer an intense chill as the Pokémon siphons their life-force away, glaring at them with its bright red eye all the while.

Name: Dusclops
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may put 5 damage counters on 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. If you use this Ability, this Pokémon is Knocked Out.
Flavor-Text: Dusclops’s true form is said to be a bright red eye burning within its body, but no one has ever seen such a thing.

Name: Dusknoir
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may put 13 damage counters on 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. If you use this Ability, this Pokémon is Knocked Out.
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon can’t retreat.
Flavor-Text: It comes and goes between this world and the afterlife. People fear this Pokémon because it is said to suck in lost spirits and take them along.

Name: Spritzee
Flavor-Text: A scent pouch within this Pokémon’s body allows it to create various scents. A change in its diet will alter the fragrance it produces.

Name: Aromatisse
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: The scent that constantly emits from its fur is so powerful that this Pokémon’s companions will eventually lose their sense of smell.

Name: Sylveon
Ability-Effect: Prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from your opponent’s Pokémon ex.
Flavor-Text: Sylveon cuts an elegant figure as it dances lightly around, feelers fluttering, but its piercing moves aim straight for its opponents’ weak spots.

Name: Sylveon ex
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, attacks used by the Defending Pokémon do 100 less damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

Choose 2 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. Shuffle those Pokémon and all attached cards into your opponent’s deck. If 1 of your Pokémon used Angelite during your last turn, this attack can’t be used.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Tera-Rule: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s).

Name: Scream Tail
Effects: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon for each damage counter on this Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: There has been only one reported sighting of this Pokémon. It resembles a mysterious creature depicted in an old expedition journal.

Name: Flutter Mane
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, your opponent’s Active Pokémon has no Abilities, except for Midnight Fluttering.
Effects: Put 2 damage counters on your opponent’s Benched Pokémon in any way you like.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon has characteristics similar to those of Flutter Mane, a creature mentioned in a certain book.

Name: Munkidori
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon has any [D] Energy attached, you may move up to 3 damage counters from 1 of your Pokémon to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon.
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: Munkidori keeps itself somewhere safe while it toys with its foes, using psychokinesis to induce intense dizziness.

Name: Fezandipiti
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon has any [D] Energy attached and is damaged by an attack, flip a coin. If heads, prevent that damage.
Effects: This attack does 30 damage for each Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Fezandipiti beats its glossy wings to scatter pheromones that captivate people and Pokémon.

Name: Iron Boulder
Effects: If you don’t have the same number of cards in your hand as your opponent, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: It was named after a mysterious object recorded in an old book. Its body seems to be metallic.

Name: Larvitar
Flavor-Text: It is born deep underground. It can’t emerge until it has entirely consumed the soil around it.

Name: Pupitar
Flavor-Text: Its shell is as hard as bedrock, and it is also very strong. Its thrashing can topple a mountain.

Name: Groudon
Effects: Attach a Basic [F] Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon.
Discard up to 4 Energy from your Pokémon. This attack does 60 damage for each card you discarded in this way.
Flavor-Text: This legendary Pokémon is said to represent the land. It went to sleep after dueling Kyogre.

Name: Riolu
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It has the peculiar power of being able to see emotions, such as joy and rage, in the form of waves.

Name: Lucario ex
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon’s Tornado Rush attack does 100 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Hippopotas
Flavor-Text: It shrouds itself in sand to ward off germs. It travels easily through the sands of the desert.

Name: Hippowdon
Flavor-Text: It brandishes its gaping mouth in a display of fearsome strength. It raises vast quantities of sand while attacking.

Name: Bloodmoon Ursaluna
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may attach up to 2 Basic [F] Energy cards from your hand to this Pokémon.
Effects: This attack does 30 more damage for each damage counter on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This special Ursaluna can see in the dark with its left eye and protects itself with mud that is as hard as iron.

Name: Great Tusk
Effects: Discard the top card of your opponent’s deck. If you played an Ancient Supporter card from your hand during this turn, discard 3 more cards in this way.
Flavor-Text: Sightings of this Pokémon have occurred in recent years. The name Great Tusk was taken from a creature listed in a certain book.

Name: Sandy Shocks ex
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if your opponent has 4 or fewer Prize cards remaining, you may attach a Basic [F] Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Okidogi
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon has any [D] Energy attached, it gets +100 HP, and the attacks it uses do 100 more damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: Okidogi is a ruffian with a short temper. It can pulverize anything by swinging around the chain on its neck.

Name: Cornerstone Mask Ogerpon ex
Ability-Effect: Prevent all damage from attacks done to this Pokémon by your opponent’s Pokémon that have an Ability.
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness or Resistance, or by any effects on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Tera-Rule: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s).

Name: Umbreon
Effects: This attack does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness or Resistance, or by any effects on that Pokémon.
During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
Flavor-Text: When exposed to the moon’s aura, the rings on its body glow faintly and it gains a mysterious power.

Name: Umbreon ex
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Discard all Energy from this Pokémon, and take a Prize card.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Tera-Rule: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s).

Name: Sneasel
Flavor-Text: This is a smart and sneaky Pokémon. A pair may work together to steal eggs by having one lure the parents away.

Name: Houndour
Flavor-Text: It uses different kinds of cries for communicating with others of its kind and for pursuing its prey.

Name: Houndoom
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Energy cards and attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: Upon hearing its eerie howls, other Pokémon get the shivers and head straight back to their nests.

Name: Tyranitar ex
Effects: This attack does 50 damage for each Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Discard a random card from your opponent’s hand.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Roaring Moon
Effects: This attack does 10 more damage for each Ancient card in your discard pile.
Flavor-Text: It is possible that this is the creature listed as Roaring Moon in an expedition journal that still holds many mysteries.

Name: Bronzor
Flavor-Text: They are found in ancient tombs. The patterns on their backs are said to be imbued with mysterious power.

Name: Bronzong
Ability-Effect: All of your Pokémon take 10 less damage from attacks from your opponent’s Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: It brought rains by opening portals to another world. It was revered as a bringer of plentiful harvests.

Name: Heatran
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
Flavor-Text: It dwells in volcanic caves. It digs in with its cross-shaped feet to crawl on ceilings and walls.

Name: Duraludon
Effects: This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its metal body is durable but prone to retaining heat. It vents this heat from the slits in its tail.

Name: Archaludon
Ability-Effect: All of your Pokémon that have [M] Energy attached have no Retreat Cost.
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
Flavor-Text: It digs holes on mountains, searching for food. It’s so durable that being caught in a cave-in won’t faze it.

Name: Dreepy
Flavor-Text: It has a habit of biting at Clauncher even though it doesn’t feed on them. This is said to be vestigial behavior from when Dreepy was alive.

Name: Drakloak
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may look at the top 2 cards of your deck and put 1 of them into your hand. Put the other card on the bottom of your deck.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon stores up energy in its lungs, then shoots it out. It takes care of Dreepy and battles alongside them until they’re all grown up.

Name: Dragapult ex
Effects: Put 6 damage counters on your opponent’s Benched Pokémon in any way you like.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Tera-Rule: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s).

Name: Eevee
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, it can evolve during your first turn or the turn you play it.
Effects: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Its genetic code is irregular. It may mutate if it is exposed to radiation from element stones.

Name: Eevee ex
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon can evolve into any Pokémon ex that evolves from Eevee if you play it from your hand onto this Pokémon. (This Pokémon can’t evolve during your first turn or the turn you play it.)
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Tera-Rule: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s).

Name: Snorlax ex
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 120 damage for each heads.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Hoothoot
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon can’t be Asleep.
Flavor-Text: Since it tells time precisely, some countries consider it to be a deity of wisdom, versed in the ways of the world.

Name: Noctowl
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, when you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, if you have any Tera Pokémon in play, you may search your deck for up to 2 Trainer cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: Its extremely soft wings make no sound in flight. It silently sneaks up on prey without being detected.

Name: Dunsparce
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent’s next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It creates mazes in dark locations. When spotted, it flees into the ground by digging with its tail.

Name: Dudunsparce
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may draw 3 cards. If you drew any cards in this way, shuffle this Pokémon and all attached cards into your deck.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon uses its hard tail to make its nest by boring holes into bedrock deep underground. The nest can reach lengths of over six miles.

Name: Miltank
Effects: You can use this attack only if this Pokémon used Rollout during your last turn.
Flavor-Text: Miltank produces highly nutritious milk, so it’s been supporting the lives of people and other Pokémon since ancient times.

Name: Lugia ex
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. For each heads, discard an Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Buneary
Flavor-Text: If both of Buneary’s ears are rolled up, something is wrong with its body or mind. It’s a sure sign the Pokémon is in need of care.

Name: Lopunny
Flavor-Text: Lopunny is constantly monitoring its surroundings. If danger approaches, this Pokémon responds with superdestructive kicks.

Name: Fan Rotom
Ability-Effect: Once during your first turn, you may search your deck for up to 3 [C] Pokémon with 100 HP or less, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck. You can’t use more than 1 Fan Call Ability during your turn.
Effects: If there is no Stadium in play, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: There are several appliances that Rotom can inspirit, but the first one to be developed was the electric fan.

Name: Regigigas
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is a Tera Pokémon, this attack does 230 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It is said to have made Pokémon that look like itself from a special ice mountain, rocks, and magma.

Name: Shaymin
Effects: Move an Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It can dissolve toxins in the air to instantly transform ruined land into a lush field of flowers.

Name: Furfrou
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon takes 20 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: There was an era when aristocrats would compete to see who could trim their Furfrou’s fur into the most exquisite style.

Name: Hawlucha
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is a Pokémon ex, this attack does 50 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Hawlucha live quietly without flocking together. They fight constantly with their natural enemies: noisy groups of Primeape.

Name: Noibat
Flavor-Text: Noibat can change the frequency of its sound waves at will, and it generates ultrasonic waves of up to 200,000 hertz.

Name: Noivern ex
Effects: You may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Terapagos ex

During your opponent’s next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from Basic non-[C] Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Amarys

Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Area Zero Underdepths

When this card leaves play, both players discard Pokémon from their Bench until they have 5, and the player who played this card discards first.
Stadium-Rule: You may play only 1 Stadium card during your turn. Put it next to the Active Spot, and discard it if another Stadium comes into play. A Stadium with the same name can’t be played.

Name: Binding Mochi
Description: Attacks used by the Poisoned Pokémon this card is attached to do 40 more damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Tool-Rule: You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.

Name: Black Belt’s Training

Description: During this turn, attacks used by your Pokémon do 40 more damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon ex (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Briar

Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Buddy-Buddy Poffin
Description: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon with 70 HP or less and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

Name: Bug Catching Set
Description: Look at the top 7 cards of your deck. You may reveal up to 2 in any combination of [G] Pokémon and Basic [G] Energy cards you find there and put them into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.

Name: Carmine
Description: If you go first, you may use this card during your first turn.Discard your hand and draw 5 cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Ciphermaniac’s Codebreaking
Description: Search your deck for 2 cards, shuffle your deck, then put those cards on top of it in any order.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Crispin
Description: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Energy cards of different types, reveal them, and put 1 of them into your hand. Attach the other to 1 of your Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Earthen Vessel
Description: You can use this card only if you discard another card from your hand.Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Explorer’s Guidance
Description: Look at the top 6 cards of your deck and put 2 of them into your hand. Discard the other cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Festival Grounds
Description: Each Pokémon that has any Energy attached (both yours and your opponent’s) recovers from all Special Conditions and can’t be affected by any Special Conditions.
Stadium-Rule: You may play only 1 Stadium card during your turn. Put it next to the Active Spot, and discard it if another Stadium comes into play. A Stadium with the same name can’t be played.

Name: Friends in Paldea
Description: Draw 3 cards.
